name: Verify the audit
summary: A compact clean rollout whose point is the record. Every action lands in a tamper-evident hash chain you can verify — and watch break when a record is altered.
moment: every action is recorded to a tamper-evident hash chain; verify passes, tamper fails

events.title: Fleet topology loaded
events.body: Three hosts under management: TALOS-CANARY (canary), and the ring1 HA pair TALOS-R1A / TALOS-R1B. All on baseline 2.1.3, all green.

events.title: Directive received
events.body: Roll out build 2.1.4 to the fleet. This run is also a compliance demonstration: every action will be written to the audit log.

data.text: Roll out 2.1.4 to the fleet, canary first, with full audit.

events.title: Audit recording enabled
events.body: Every action the agents take — each deploy, each gate evaluation, each approval — is appended to a tamper-evident hash chain. Each record's hash covers its own contents plus the hash of the record before it, so any later edit breaks every link downstream.

data.text: All actions are being recorded to a tamper-evident, hash-chained audit log.

events.title: Staged rollout authored
events.body: Canary TALOS-CANARY first, soak, gate. On pass, request ring1 approval, then deploy TALOS-R1A and TALOS-R1B one host at a time.

data.rationale: Standard canary-first staging with sequential HA-pair updates. Compact soak windows for the audit demonstration.

events.title: Plan approved
events.body: Conservative, canary-first, sequential HA-pair updates. Approved. The approval itself is recorded.

reasons: Every step will be captured in the audit chain.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-CANARY → 2.1.4
events.body: Executor pushes 2.1.4 to TALOS-CANARY. Audit record written: 'deploy TALOS-CANARY 2.1.3 -> 2.1.4'.

events.title: TALOS-CANARY healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: Service running, HTTP 200, memory nominal. Canary green on the new build.

events.title: Canary soak
events.body: 60s soak on TALOS-CANARY under the canary gate.

events.title: Canary gate: PASS
events.body: TALOS-CANARY clears the canary gate — memory 816MB, HTTP 200, 1 error, service Running. Audit record written: 'gate_eval TALOS-CANARY canary_gate -> pass'.

events.title: Approval required: ring1
events.body: Canary passed. Ring1 promotion needs explicit human approval. The request and the grant are both recorded.

data.prompt: Canary-01 cleared the gate on 2.1.4. Approve promotion to production ring1 (TALOS-R1A, TALOS-R1B)?

events.title: Promotion approved
events.body: Operator approved. Audit record written: 'approval ring1 -> granted'. Executor proceeds with the HA pair, one host at a time.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1A → 2.1.4
events.body: First HA-pair member. Audit record written: 'deploy TALOS-R1A 2.1.3 -> 2.1.4'.

events.title: TALOS-R1A healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1A green on 2.1.4; partner TALOS-R1B still green on 2.1.3.

events.title: Deploying TALOS-R1B → 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1A confirmed healthy, so the Executor updates TALOS-R1B. Audit record written: 'deploy TALOS-R1B 2.1.3 -> 2.1.4'.

events.title: TALOS-R1B healthy on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-R1B green on 2.1.4. Both ring members now run the new build.

events.title: Ring gate: PASS
events.body: Both ring1 members clear the ring gate. Audit record written: 'gate_eval TALOS-R1B ring_gate -> pass'. The rollout is complete and fully logged.

events.title: Fleet all green on 2.1.4
events.body: TALOS-CANARY, TALOS-R1A, TALOS-R1B all green on 2.1.4. Seven actions were taken; seven records are in the chain.

events.title: Now verify the chain
events.body: Open the Audit panel and click Verify: the server recomputes every hash from GENESIS forward and confirms the chain is intact. Then click Tamper a record — the server flips one record's action, recomputes, and the chain breaks at exactly that record and stays broken for every record after it. That is what tamper-evident means: you cannot quietly rewrite history.

data.text: Verify → chain OK. Tamper a record → chain FAILS at that record. The audit log cannot be silently edited.

events.title: Done — every action accounted for
events.body: Build 2.1.4 shipped clean, and every action the agents took is sealed in a tamper-evident hash chain. Verify passes today; if anyone alters a record after the fact, Verify fails at the altered link. Honest fleet (simulated), real agents, real auditability.

data.summary: 2.1.4 deployed to all 3 hosts; 7 actions recorded; hash chain verifiable and tamper-evident.
